name: AXS Compression Fakeout Reexpansion v1
edge_mechanism: compression fakeout followed by directional reclaim and re-expansion
thesis: AXS 4h is the structurally different lane for a compression fakeout idea because fake releases and violent repairs should be more visible there. The edge comes from ignoring the first fake move and entering on directional reclaim when expansion resumes.
invalidation_condition: Abort if fakeout/reclaim sequences are too sparse, if the reclaim lacks a second expansion leg, or if AXS only generates untradable volatility noise.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Compression resolves the wrong way first, snaps back through value, and then expands in the repaired direction with enough range to support partials plus a runner.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transitioning chop often prints false initial expansions before the real move. AXS should amplify that behavior if the mechanism is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 4h is a good stress-test lane because the mechanism should either be obvious there or fail quickly; ETH and TAO provide sanity checks.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the first fake move is followed by full reversion instead of repaired expansion, the runner logic never gets paid.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but the runner underperforms, convert more size to early harvesting before changing compression logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Mixed hit rate with a few outsized repaired expansions carrying expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: The setup appears across AXS and at least one cross-check lane with enough post-cost range to justify partial-plus-runner management.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, fakeouts with no real repaired leg, or only cosmetic low-trade PF mirages.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept reasonable by broad fakeout/reclaim logic

validation_basket.why_included: Most expressive stress-test lane for fakeout-repair behavior in the current basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: The pattern prints enough repaired expansions to justify the management structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS only produces chaos with no repaired second leg.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sharp fake release, fast reclaim, then a meaningful second expansion leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives on another volatile lane rather than only AXS.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO shows the same repaired-expansion shape with acceptable structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only appears on AXS and disappears elsewhere.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner fakeout-repair expansions than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Determines whether the mechanism generalizes to a cleaner similar-asset lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH still produces tradable repaired expansions, even with lower amplitude.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Mechanism fully vanishes on the cleaner lane.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Smaller but more orderly repaired expansions.
